ALMODIS de la Marche (-murdered 16 Oct …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Almodis de la Marche (c. 1020-16 October 1071) was a French noble. She was famed for her marriage career, in particularly for her third marriage to Ramon Berenguer I, Count of Barcelona, with whom she committed double bigamy in 1053, for which the Pope had them excommunicated.

«b»Life«/b»
She was the daughter of Bernard I, Count of Marche and wife Amélie.

She married Hugh V of Lusignan around 1038 and they had two sons and one daughter. Almodis and Hugh of Lusignan divorced due to consanguinity, and Hugh arranged for her to marry Count Pons of Toulouse in 1040. She was still Pons' wife in April 1053, but shortly thereafter Almodis was abducted by Ramon Berenguer I, Count of Barcelona. He kidnapped her from Narbonne with the aid of a fleet sent north by his ally, the Muslim emir of Tortosa. They married immediately (despite the fact both of her previous husbands were still alive) and they appear with their twin sons in a charter the next year. Pope Victor II excommunicated Almodis and Ramon for this illegal marriage until 1056.

Almodis maintained contact with her former husbands and many children, and in 1066/1067 she traveled to Toulouse for her daughter's wedding. A few years before, in 1060, Hugh V of Lusignan had revolted against his lord, Duke William VIII of Aquitaine, in support of Almodis' son William IV of Toulouse. Her sons supported one another in military campaigns; Hugh VI of Lusignan, Raymond IV of Toulouse, and Berenguer Ramon all took the Cross.

Her third husband Ramon was married to her sister Rangearde de la Marche's daughter Isabela Trencavel. Their son, Peter Raymundi, was Ramon's original heir. Raymundi/Pere-Ramon apparently resented Almodis' influence and was concerned she was trying to replace him with her own two sons, his consanguinous nephews, who had both his late mother's and his step-mother's claims through their father, Count La Marche. He murdered her in October 1071.

Pere-Ramon was disinherited and exiled for his crime wherefore he fled the country. When his father died in 1076, Barcelona was split between Berenguer Ramon and Ramon Berenguer, Almodis' sons. The family history of murder did not end with Pere-Ramon, as Berenguer Ramon earned his nickname "The Fratricide" when he killed his own twin brother.

She married Hugh V of Lusignan around 1038 and they had two sons and one daughter:

1.) Hugh VI of Lusignan (c. 1039-1101)

2.) Jordan de Lusignan

3.) Mélisende de Lusignan (b. bef. 1055), married before 1074 to Simon I "l'Archevêque", Vidame de Parthenay

Almodis and Hugh of Lusignan divorced due to consanguinity, and Hugh arranged for her to marry Count Pons of Toulouse in 1040. Together they produced several children, including:

1.) William IV of Toulouse

2.) Raymond IV of Toulouse

3.) Hugh, Abbot of Saint-Gilles

4.) Almodis of Toulouse, married Count Pierre of Melgueil

She was still Pons' wife in April 1053, but shortly thereafter Almodis was abducted by Ramon Berenguer I, Count of Barcelona. He kidnapped her from Narbonne with the aid of a fleet sent north by his ally, the Muslim emir of Tortosa. They married immediately (despite the fact both of her previous husbands were still alive) and they appear with their twin sons in a charter the next year. Pope Victor II excommunicated Almodis and Ramon for this illegal marriage until 1056. 

Together they produced four children:

1.) Berenguer Ramon II, Count of Barcelona

2.) Ramon Berenguer II, Count of Barcelona

3.) Agnes of Barcelona, married Count Guigues II of Albon

4.) Sancha of Barcelona, married Count Guillermo Ramon I of Cerdagne

Almodis maintained contact with her former husbands and many children, and in 1066/1067 she traveled to Toulouse for her daughter's wedding. A few years before, in 1060, Hugh V of Lusignan had revolted against his lord, Duke William VIII of Aquitaine, in support of Almodis' son William IV of Toulouse. Her sons supported one another in military campaigns; Hugh VI of Lusignan, Raymond IV of Toulouse, and Berenguer Ramon all took the Cross.

Her third husband Ramon was married to her sister Rangearde de la Marche's daughter Isabela Trencavel. Their son, Peter Raymundi, was Ramon's original heir. Raymundi/Pere-Ramon apparently resented Almodis' influence and was concerned she was trying to replace him with her own two sons, his consanguinous nephews, who had both his late mother's and his step-mother's claims through their father, Count La Marche. He murdered her in October 1071. Pere-Ramon was disinherited and exiled for his crime wherefore he fled the country. When his father died in 1076, Barcelona was split between Berenguer Ramon and Ramon Berenguer, Almodis' sons. The family history of murder did not end with Pere-Ramon, as Berenguer Ramon earned his nickname "The Fratricide" when he killed his own twin brother.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Almodis de La Marché於1020年出生在法國米迪-列內斯豪-加洛圖勞斯時，她的父親Bernard I Comte de la Marche28歲，母親Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche20歲。她於1034年與Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1071年10月16日在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿享年51歲，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Marché
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: March
meanings: English: habitational name from March (Cambridgeshire) or from any of numerous minor English placenames deriving from Middle English marche ‘boundary’ (partly from Old English mearc in the dative-case form mearce, partly from Old French marche). Compare Mark 2.
Catalan: from the personal name March, Catalan equivalent of Mark 1. This surname is also found in southern France.
German: topographic name from Middle Low German march, Middle High German marc(h) ‘mark, sign, boundary’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word (Bavaria).

en.lifeSummary: When Almodis de La Marché was born in 1020, in Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, France, her father, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, was 28 and her mother, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, was 20. She married Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé in 1034. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 16 October 1071, in Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain, at the age of 51, and was buried in Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Almodis de La Marché est née en 1020, à Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, France, son père, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé en 1034. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 16 octobre 1071, à Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Almodis de La Marché 1020 in Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Bernard I Comte de la Marche 28 und ihre Mutter Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche 20. Sie hat 1034 Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 16. Oktober 1071 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Barcelona, Catalunya, Spanien und wurde in Barcelona, Catalunya, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Almodis de La Marché è nata il nel 1020 nel Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, Francia, sua padre, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé nel 1034. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 ottobre 1071, nel Barcelona, Catalunya, Spagna, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Barcelona, Catalunya, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Almodis de La Marchéが1020年にフランスミディ-プイルeンeスハウテ-ガーオントウロウゼで生まれた時，父Bernard I Comte de la Marcheは28歳，母Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marcheは20歳であった。1034年にHugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhéと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1071年10月16日にスペインカタルニーアバーセロナで51歳で死去し，スペインカタルニーアバーセロナにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Almodis de La Marché(이)가 1020년 프랑스 미디-p이ee스 하테-가니 토우루스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bernard I Comte de la Marche(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1034년 Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1071년 10월 16일 스페인 카털니아 바셀나에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카털니아 바셀나에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Almodis de La Marché nasceu em 1020, em Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, França, seu pai, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé em 1034. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 16 de outubro de 1071, em Barcelona, Catalunya, Espanha, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Barcelona, Catalunya, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Almodis de La Marché родилась в 1020 г. в местности тоулоус, хауте-гаронн, миди-пирeнeесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Bernard I Comte de la Marche было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé в 1034 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 16 октября 1071 г. в местности барселона, каталуня, Испания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности барселона, каталуня, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Almodis de La Marché nació en 1020, en Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Mediodía-Pirineos, Francia, su papá, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Hugh de Lusignan V, Seigneur de Lusignan et de Couhé en 1034. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de octubre de 1071, en Barcelona, Cataluña, España, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Barcelona, Cataluña, España.
